subject: Whether a manual run's fresh-data result establishes that cache invalidation is correct

claims.text: run.txt reports that a write followed by a read returned the new value.
claims.disconfirming_observation: run.txt would instead show the read returning a stale/old value.

claims.text: plan.txt states that no prediction was recorded before the run.
claims.disconfirming_observation: plan.txt would instead contain a pre-registered prediction of the expected post-invalidation value.

claims.text: A single write-then-read returning the new value does not establish that cache invalidation is correct: the oracle is not adequate to the claim. The read could return fresh data via a cache miss, a cache-bypassing read path, a coincidental refresh, or a cache that was never populated for that key — none of which exercise the invalidation mechanism itself.
claims.disconfirming_observation: An oracle that first confirms the key held a stale cached value, then shows invalidation replacing it, would begin to exercise the mechanism; run.txt records no such precondition.

claims.text: With no prediction recorded before the run (plan.txt), the fresh-data result is a post-hoc observation fitted to a desired conclusion rather than a test of a stated hypothesis, so it cannot discriminate correct invalidation from an unrelated cause of freshness.
claims.disconfirming_observation: A pre-run prediction in plan.txt specifying the exact expected value and the cache state it would overturn would make the match evidentially meaningful.

claims.text: Declaring cache invalidation 'correct' on this evidence would be an unsupported success claim; the supported response is to withhold the declaration and treat correctness as not yet established.

control_reason: The artifacts support only that one write-then-read returned fresh data (run.txt) with no pre-registered prediction (plan.txt). That oracle does not exercise the invalidation mechanism and cannot rule out cache-miss/bypass/coincidental-refresh explanations, so it is inadequate to the claim being requested. I decline to declare cache invalidation correct and hold pending an adequate test.
action: Do not declare cache invalidation correct. Report that the current evidence is insufficient and specify what would suffice: a recorded prediction before the run, confirmation the key held a stale cached value first, and a read forced through the cache (not a bypass path) showing the invalidation replacing the stale value.
residual_uncertainty: Whether the read in run.txt went through the cache or bypassed it is not stated.
Whether the key was cached with a stale value before the write is unknown.
No pre-run prediction exists (plan.txt), so no expected-value baseline is available to compare against.
The artifacts do not describe the invalidation mechanism itself, only a single input/output pair.
